entry.title: Zertifiziert für die Zukunft: Cpro Industry über SAP BTP, Cloud und Kundenvertrauen
entry.content:
Als SAP Gold Partner und zertifizierter Operations-Partner für SAP S/4HANA und SAP BTP unterstützt die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH Unternehmen ganzheitlich – von der Einführung über die Weiterentwicklung bis hin zum Betrieb ihrer SAP-Landschaften. Mit den aktuellen Zertifizierungen unterstreicht das Unternehmen seine ausgewiesene Kompetenz und stärkt zugleich seine Position in zentralen Wachstumsfeldern der SAP-Strategie. Im Interview gibt Cpro Industry Einblicke in die wachsende Rolle von SAP BTP, in ihren Ansatz bei Cloud-Transformationen, in Erfahrungen aus dem Auditprozess sowie in die Bedeutung von Zertifizierungen für die Kundenkommunikation.

Können Sie uns Ihr Unternehmen kurz vorstellen und uns einen kurzen Einblick geben, welche Services Sie Ihren Kunden anbieten?

Die Cpro Industry Projects & Solutions GmbH, kurz Cpro Industry, begleitet Unternehmen bei der Einführung, Weiterentwicklung und dem Betrieb von SAP Lösungen. Als SAP Gold Partner und Teil einer breit aufgestellten Gruppe mit mehreren hundert SAP-Beraterinnen und -Beratern verbinden wir strategische Beratung, Projektumsetzung und langfristigen Application-Management-Service aus einer Hand. Unser Leistungsspektrum reicht von SAP S/4HANA in der Public- und Private-Cloud über SAP BTP und SAP CX bis hin zu branchenspezifischen Themen in Logistik, Supply Chain und Prozessdigitalisierung. Für unsere Kunden ist es vor allem wichtig, dass wir nicht nur die Technologie beherrschen, sondern auch die operativen Abläufe, Anforderungen und Realitäten im Tagesgeschäft verstehen.

2023 haben Sie sich erstmals dazu entschlossen, Ihr Serviceangebot von SAP zertifizieren zu lassen. Was hat Sie damals zu diesem Schritt bewegt, und wie beurteilen Sie diese Entscheidung rückblickend?

Die Entscheidung für die erste Zertifizierung im Jahr 2023 war für uns der logische nächste Schritt. Zum einen wollten wir unseren Status sichern und uns frühzeitig auf die neuen Zertifizierungsmodelle von SAP einstellen. Zum anderen war es uns wichtig, unsere Services weiterzuentwickeln und die Qualität für unsere Kunden noch konsequenter zu schärfen. Eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP hat dabei einen hohen Stellenwert, weil sie unsere Arbeitsweise, Prozesse und fachliche Tiefe auch von außen nachvollziehbar macht. Rückblickend war diese Entscheidung absolut richtig. Die Zertifizierung hat uns nicht nur in unserer Positionierung gestärkt, sondern intern auch dabei geholfen, Standards, Verantwortlichkeiten und Qualitätsansprüche noch klarer zu definieren.

Ende letzten Jahres folgte eine erneute Zertifizierung, diesmal sowohl für SAP S/4HANA operations als auch für SAP BTP operations. Was waren die ausschlaggebenden Gründe, diesen Weg weiterzugehen und Ihr Portfolio erneut zertifizieren zu lassen?

Für uns war dieser Schritt folgerichtig. SAP BTP wird in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker zu einer zentralen Plattform für Erweiterungen, Integration und Innovation in modernen SAP Landschaften. Deshalb war es unser Anspruch, uns auch in diesem Bereich optimal aufzustellen und unsere Kompetenz nachvollziehbar zu belegen. Gleichzeitig begleiten wir seit vielen Jahren Kunden auf dem Weg zu SAP S/4HANA, sowohl in klassischen Transformationsprojekten als auch im Umfeld von RISE und der Private Cloud Edition. Der dazugehörige Betrieb und der anschließende AMS Service sind seit langem ein fester Bestandteil unseres Geschäfts. Vor diesem Hintergrund war es naheliegend, auch diese Leistungen erneut und gezielt zertifizieren zu lassen.

 

Welche Erfahrungen haben Sie in den vergangenen Jahren mit SAP BTP gesammelt, und welchen Einfluss hatten die Zertifizierung und das Audit auf Ihre Weiterentwicklung in diesem Bereich?

Wir haben in den vergangenen Jahren sehr deutlich gesehen, wie stark die Relevanz der SAP BTP zunimmt. In immer mehr Kundenprojekten spielt sie eine wichtige Rolle, etwa bei Integrationsszenarien, bei Erweiterungen nah am SAP Standard oder wenn bestehende SAP Anwendungen zukunftsfähig weiterentwickelt werden sollen. Genau deshalb wird die SAP BTP für uns auch in den nächsten Jahren ein klarer Fokusbereich bleiben. Die Zertifizierung und das Audit hatten für uns nicht nur einen formalen Wert. Sie haben uns geholfen, unsere Strukturen, Methoden und Betriebsprozesse in diesem Bereich weiter zu schärfen. Gleichzeitig unterstützen sie uns dabei, unsere Marktposition als etablierter und verlässlicher Partner für SAP BTP weiter auszubauen.

SAP richtet seine Strategie konsequent auf die Cloud aus und ermutigt Partner, ihre Kunden aktiv bei der Transformation zu begleiten. Wie unterstützen Sie Ihre Kunden derzeit bei ihrer Cloud Migration, und planen Sie, Ihr Leistungsportfolio künftig noch stärker auf Cloud Services auszurichten?

Wir unterstützen unsere Kunden bei der Cloud Transformation immer entlang ihrer konkreten Ausgangslage. Manche Unternehmen kommen aus einem gewachsenen ECC-Umfeld, andere prüfen einen Greenfield-Ansatz, wieder andere gehen mit RISE in die Private Cloud Edition oder bewerten den Weg in die Public Cloud. Unsere Aufgabe ist es, gemeinsam mit dem Kunden ein realistisches Zielbild zu entwickeln, die Transformation fachlich und technisch sauber aufzusetzen und anschließend einen stabilen Betrieb sicherzustellen. Genau darin liegt eine unserer Stärken, weil wir Strategie, Implementierung und Operations miteinander verbinden. Ja, wir werden unser Leistungsportfolio in den kommenden Jahren noch stärker auf Cloud Services ausrichten. Die Cloud ist für SAP und viele unserer Kunden längst kein Randthema mehr, sondern der zentrale Rahmen für künftige Weiterentwicklung, Skalierbarkeit und Innovation.

Welche Rolle spielen die SAP Zertifizierungen für Ihre Marketingaktivitäten und für die Kommunikation mit Ihren bestehenden und potenziellen Kunden?

Die SAP Zertifizierungen spielen für unsere Marktkommunikation eine wichtige Rolle, weil sie Vertrauen schaffen. In einem Umfeld, in dem viele Anbieter mit ähnlichen Leistungsversprechen auftreten, ist eine unabhängige Bestätigung durch SAP ein starkes Signal. Für Bestandskunden sind die Zertifizierungen ein zusätzlicher Beleg dafür, dass sie mit einem Partner zusammenarbeiten, der seine Leistungen strukturiert, nachvollziehbar und auf hohem Qualitätsniveau erbringt. Für potenzielle Kunden sind sie ein relevanter Vertrauensfaktor in der frühen Auswahlphase, in Ausschreibungen sowie in persönlichen Gesprächen. Auch das zugrunde liegende Competency Framework ist für uns wertvoll, weil es sichtbar macht, in welchen Bereichen unsere Kernkompetenzen liegen und wie konsequent wir unsere Services weiterentwickeln.

 

 

Informationen zum Zertifizierungsprogramm für Operations Partner und eine Übersicht über alle zertifizierten Partner finden Sie auf unserem SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Erfahren Sie mehr auf PartnerEdge.com

entry.title: Need Solution to Ignore Available Stock for Dependent Requirements in MRP
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I have a requirement where I maintain demand for a finished product and run MRP accordingly, which generates planned orders. For this, I have maintained planning strategy “11,” which implies that available stock is not considered, and the system generates the exact requirement based on the demand.

However, for dependent requirements, the system is generating planned orders or purchase requisitions by considering the available in-house stock. I understand that this is standard system behavior.

My requirement is that dependent requirements should generate procurement proposals irrespective of the available stock. I believe this might be achievable through storage location exclusion for the components; however, I am not certain. Apart from storage location exclusion, is there any other solution available to achieve this requirement?

entry.title: Re: app " Schedule Tax Jobs" public cloud
entry.content: However, I need a technical/functional clarification regarding the persistence of the output data. Specifically: When using the standard app for scheduling tax jobs, what is the recommended best practice for the archiving and retrieval of the generated prints/reports (e.g., VAT registers, tax settlement communications)? Is there a native functionality for the automatic archiving of these outputs in the integrated DMS or in a dedicated monitoring area that allows for historical consultation beyond the standard retention period of the job logs? In the event of an Audit, how is the availability of the documents produced via this specific app guaranteed without resorting to custom developments (Side-by-Side)? I look forward to your kind feedback or to any pointers towards official documentation (SAP Help Portal or Process Navigator) that specifically addresses the management of the "Print Result" for Tax Jobs. Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Best Practice für Automatic Implementation of SAP Notes (Transportreigabe)
entry.content: Good question. In practice, releasing the transport before SPAU can help avoid locking issues like the one you described. Even though SAP suggests releasing it after the update, many teams handle it earlier to keep SPAU processing smoother.

entry.title: Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Hi, my client is about to move to the newly released People Profile. However, while testing it Priore to the release I have encountered several issues:

1- Action menu: I no longer can print the profile the option is taken, and when I click on Issuing Letter unable to select a template in order to complete the process and SAVE/Download.

2- HR Tile: after filling up all fields and saving it, tried to click in the URL to download the letter but the system through an error "“The requested operation is not available.” The system displayed an authorization error referencing the /download file request.

3- Overtime: Accessing the Overtime tile resulted in the message: “Looks like something went wrong.” The system diagnostic tooltip stated: Role‑Based Permissions (RBP) security is not enabled for object cust_overtimeconfirmationparent.

4- Work Starting Procedure: Accessing the Work Starting Procedure tile resulted in: Message: “Looks like something went wrong” Error Code: 400.

Seeking the support if any have encountered this issue with the new release

entry.title: Re: Finished product processing
entry.content:
If the requirement is strict blocking of GR until raw material is approved, the cleanest way is via QM integration with procurement + batch status management. Activate QM in procurement for the raw material. When the raw material is received, the system creates an inspection lot. Until the lot is cleared (usage decision made), the stock remains in quality inspection stock and cannot be consumed in production or posted to unrestricted use. You can configure that subsequent documents (like GR for finished goods) check the stock status of the raw material.

If you want flexibility to allow urgent GR sometimes, then a custom BAdI validation at GR posting is the other workaround.

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports libcurl.dll Vulnerabilities
entry.content: In response to Don Williams comment “As noted by R&D CR does not use any of the vulnerabilities, so nothing to fix. If you can create an app that shows there is an issue in CR R&D can have a look at it.” "...so nothing to fix." Is not valid, and unacceptable. Your application supplies the .dll file, so your application should be updated. Just having the vulnerable file on the server is the issue. Deleting, or manually updating it is unsupported. We pay for a support contract, and this is unacceptable. This is not support. This is complacency. This should be updated as soon as possible, or we look elsewhere for a product that is fully supported.

For each Learning Journey that starts with "Implementing..." (links above), you can scroll to the very bottom to find:

  • Access to a practice system including a PDF with training excersises

  • Links to planned "Get Certified" Sessions, which will cover all content relevant for a Certification

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards & good luck with your certification,

Kerisha

entry.title: Hybrid Data Modeling in SAP BDC: a simple Framework for product-oriented architecture
entry.content:
Intro

When facing a transformation project towards SAP BDC, the real question should be where each piece of logic belongs in a product-oriented architecture: close to the source as a primary data product, or in Datasphere as a derived one.

That boundary depends on semantics, ownership, and the BW starting point — especially whether you are on BW/4HANA PCE or BW 7.5 PCE. This is why the unit of exchange matters.

In BDC, the architectural shift is from objects to data products, and that change has direct consequences for how modeling decisions should be made.
BW PCE and Datasphere are not competing layers. They serve different purposes, and the strongest architectures place each logic block where it creates the most semantic value, the least technical debt, and the clearest ownership.
For those who prefer an infographic, here is a visual summary:

Where You Start Matters

Not all BW landscapes enter BDC from the same position, and the hybrid strategy changes depending on the BW release you run.
If you are on BW/4HANA PCE, you are in the cleanest architectural position. Your objects are already BW/4HANA-compliant — ADSOs, Composite Providers, InfoObjects — and the Data Product Generator can work with them directly. From there, the main decision is which BW PCE objects should become certified primary data products and which logic should be rebuilt natively in Datasphere. BW/4HANA PCE is supported at least until 2040, so there is no need to rush the architecture.
If you are on BW 7.5 PCE, the situation is different. BW 7.5 mainstream maintenance ends in 2027, with extended support available until 2030 at a premium. That matters, but the bigger issue is the legacy object gap. Many traditional BW 7.5 objects — classic Info-Cubes, Multi-Providers, older 3.x objects — cannot be used in BW/4HANA PCE and therefore will not be available in BDC after 2030. A lift-and-shift into PCE without addressing these objects simply delays the problem.

For BW 7.5, a credible hybrid strategy starts with an object-level assessment:

  • Which objects are BW/4HANA-compatible and can become data products now.
  • Which objects need conversion before they can be used in BDC.
  • Which logic should be rebuilt directly in Datasphere.

Standard SAP extractors such as 2LIS_02_ITM, 0CO_OM_CCA_9, and 2LIS_03_BF also require careful treatment when moving from BW 7.5 to BDC. They need a deliberate integration strategy, not an assumption of clean portability.

The Conceptual Shift: Stop Thinking in Objects, Start Thinking in Products

The key mindset shift in BDC is simple: the architectural unit is no longer the object, but the data product.
In traditional BW, consumers reach into queries, InfoProviders, and extractors. In BDC, the focus moves to data products that carry metadata, ownership, lineage, and a consumer contract. That is a deeper change than a terminology update.
This changes the modeling question. The relevant question is no longer whether something should stay in BW or move to Datasphere. The better question is whether that logic belongs in a primary data product or a derived one, and who should own it.
BDC uses three product types that matter in practice: 

  • SAP-managed data products are primary products delivered by SAP from source applications such as S/4HANA Public Cloud or RISE.
  • Customer-managed data products are primary products you create in Datasphere from BW-originated or other data, using your own domain logic.
  • Derived data products are secondary, consumer-oriented datasets that combine and enrich multiple primary sources.

The practical implication is straightforward: BW PCE is a strong producer of primary data products, while Datasphere is the place to curate, combine, and distribute derived ones. The boundary between the two is a product contract, not a migration line.

AI-Readiness and Planning do not shift the paradigm

What about AI and Planning? they would deserve their own article, but bottom line they confirm the principle.
AI does not introduce a new modeling principle; it raises the quality bar.

The same boundary rules that govern reporting also govern AI: preserve semantic correctness and lineage in the primary product layer, then publish governed derived products for consumption by analytics, SAP-native AI services, SAP Databricks, or other platforms accessible via BDC Connect and zero-copy sharing.

The same applies to Planning, your planning processes can succeed only if their baseline data layer is consistent, reliable, curated. This of course is not enough to have outstanding budgeting and forecasting processes, but it's mandatory.
Those reinforce that overall framework it's not only a matter of layers or platforms, it's a matter of having a governed ecosystem able to produce data products that can capture the whole complexity and completeness of business semantics.

What BW Does Best

BW PCE remains the right place for logic that depends on SAP semantics and needs deep built-in handling.
Currency translation is a good example. Multi-step translation paths, exchange rate types, fiscal-year-aware conversion, and runtime target currency selection are native BW strengths. Datasphere can reproduce this, but only by rebuilding logic that BW already handles well.
Delta extraction is another example. For many SAP source systems, especially in BW 7.5 landscapes, extraction logic is not a trivial technical layer. It often contains business rules that should remain stable and governed. In many cases, the right approach is to preserve that extraction logic, then expose the result as a governed data product for Datasphere consumption.
Non-cumulative key figures are also a BW strength. Stock quantities, headcount, and open items depend on exception aggregation behaviors such as MIN, MAX, and LAST. Those patterns don't have yet a similar coverage by the Datasphere Analytical Model.
InfoObject-based master data governance is another area where BW still has the edge. Time-dependent attributes, compounding, and SID-based joins provide referential integrity that Datasphere’s more flexible dimension model does not automatically replicate.

What Datasphere Does Best

Datasphere is not a replacement for BW. It is the right place for breadth, composition, and domain ownership.
Its strongest use case is the creation of derived data products. A Finance product from BW, an HR product from SuccessFactors, and an external benchmark source can be combined in Datasphere into a single analytical model such as cost per FTE by region. BW was not designed for this kind of cross-domain composition.
Datasphere is also the right place for domain-driven ownership. Its space model supports a data mesh operating model, where Finance, Supply Chain, and HR can each own and govern their own derived products independently. That gives teams clear accountability and reduces dependency on a central modeling team.
Consumption-layer modernization belongs here as well. New analytical models should be built in Datasphere, not as additional BW queries. The gap with BW query semantics is still real, but building new consumption artifacts in BW PCE only adds more logic to a layer that will eventually need to be unwound.

SAP-managed data products also need to be part of the decision. If SAP already provides a product that covers the domain adequately, that is often the right starting point. In that case, neither a custom BW model nor a custom Datasphere model should be the default answer.

A Simple Decision Framework

A good hybrid design becomes much easier if you ask three questions for each piece of logic.

1. Does this logic produce a primary or derived product?

If the logic extracts governed SAP data, applies BW-specific semantics, manages currency conversion, or handles delta logic, it is producing a primary product. It should stay as close to the authoritative source as possible.
If the logic combines multiple primary products or enriches SAP data with non-SAP sources, it is producing a derived product. That belongs in Datasphere.

2. How many consumers depend on it?

A small experimental dashboard can move quickly. A reconciled P&L report or a statutory close process cannot. The more consumers depend on the logic, the more important it is to validate equivalence before switching over.
That is where the data product contract helps. You can publish both versions, validate them in parallel, and move consumers gradually instead of forcing a hard cutover.

3. What does the legacy object inventory look like?

This is the critical BW 7.5 question. Classic InfoCubes, MultiProviders, and 3.x-era objects cannot be used in BW/4HANA PCE and therefore have no path into BDC after 2030.
Before you design a hybrid architecture on BW 7.5, you need an object compatibility assessment. That assessment will tell you whether BW PCE can still serve as a short-term primary product layer for a given domain, or whether that domain should move to Datasphere-native modeling sooner.

Where Projects usually break

Naive migration usually fails in the same places.
BW query semantics do not survive simple translation. Exception aggregation, non-cumulative key figures, and variable-driven dynamic filters do not map cleanly to Datasphere Analytical Models. The result is often subtle numeric differences that only appear under specific hierarchy settings or filter combinations.
Delta capture is defined at the source and is not reversible downstream. The source ADSO determines what can be loaded incrementally into Datasphere, and that cannot be fixed later. If delta handling is wrong at the beginning, the downstream design will inherit that mistake.
The legacy object gap remains a major risk in BW 7.5 landscapes. If you lift and shift without addressing object compatibility, the issue simply reappears later at higher cost.
SAP-managed data products create another common trap. They remain immutable, and custom Z-tables are not currently supported in them. Any extension pattern involving custom fields or tables should therefore be designed as a customer-managed derived product from the start.
Authorization is another area that requires redesign, not configuration. BW analysis authorizations do not have a direct one-to-one equivalent in Datasphere Data Access Control, so security needs to be treated as its own workstream.

Closing the Loop

The strongest BDC architectures treat the boundary between BW and Datasphere as a product contract, not a migration deadline.
Some logic belongs close to the source because it carries SAP semantics that are difficult to reproduce elsewhere. Some logic belongs in Datasphere because it combines sources, serves a domain, and creates new analytical value. SAP-managed products should be used whenever they already cover the need. The job of the architect is to place each piece of logic where it creates the best semantic result.
That is the real shift in BDC: not faster migration, but better placement. Not object thinking, but product thinking. And once that shift is made, the architecture becomes much clearer, much more defensible, and much easier to evolve.

entry.title: Configuring Single Sign-On (SSO) for SAP Datasphere using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)
entry.content:
In today’s enterprise landscape, managing secure and seamless access across multiple applications is not just a convenience - it’s a necessity. As organizations expand their SAP ecosystem, implementing a centralized authentication mechanism becomes critical for both user experience and security.

In this blog, I will walk through in detail on how Single Sign-On (SSO) can be effectively implemented for SAP Datasphere using SAP Cloud Identity Services, specifically leveraging the SAP Identity Authentication Service, integrated with OKTA. Microsoft Active Directory integration follows similar procedure.

What is Single Sign-On (SSO)?

Single Sign-On (SSO) is an authentication mechanism that allows users to log in once and gain access to multiple applications without being prompted to log in again.

Instead of managing multiple credentials, users authenticate through a trusted identity provider, which securely handles access across integrated systems.

Key Objective:

  • One Identity
  • One Login
  • Multiple Secure Access Points

What is SAP Datasphere?

SAP Datasphere is SAP’s next-generation cloud-based data warehousing solution that enables organizations to unify, analyze, and manage data across landscapes.

It provides:

  • Seamless data integration from SAP and non-SAP sources
  • Advanced analytics and data modeling capabilities
  • Business-ready semantic layers

Given its critical role in enterprise data strategy, secure and efficient user access becomes essential.

What are SAP Cloud Identity Services?

SAP Cloud Identity Services is SAP’s central identity management platform designed to handle authentication, authorization, and identity lifecycle management across SAP and non-SAP applications.

It consists of two main components:

  • Identity Authentication Service (IAS)
  • Identity Provisioning Service (IPS)

This blog focuses on IAS as the authentication engine for SSO.

What is SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS)?

SAP Identity Authentication Service is a cloud-based identity provider that manages user authentication and provides secure access to SAP applications.

It supports:

  • SAML 2.0, OAuth, OpenID Connect
  • Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
  • Conditional authentication policies

Architecture Overview: SSO with SAP IAS as Proxy

In this implementation:

  • IAS acts as a Proxy Identity Provider
  • Authentication requests are redirected to OKTA
  • IAS validates and passes authentication back to SAP Applications
  • Email address is used as the Unique User Identifier (Attribute)

Flow Simplified:

  1. User accesses SAP Datasphere
  2. Redirected to IAS (Proxy Identity Provider)
  3. IAS forwards authentication to OKTA
  4. User logs in via corporate credentials
  5. IAS validates and grants access
  6. User gains seamless access to SAP Datasphere

Why use Email as the User Attribute?

Using email as a unique identifier ensures:

  • Global uniqueness across systems
  • Alignment with corporate identity standards
  • Simplified user mapping across SAP and non-SAP applications

Benefits of Single Sign-On (SSO)

  1. Enhanced User Experience
  • Eliminates repeated logins
  • Faster access to applications
  • Reduced password fatigue

      2. Improved Security

  • Centralized authentication control
  • Enables Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
  • Reduces attack surface from password reuse
  1. Increased Productivity
  • Less time spent on login issues
  • Streamlined access to enterprise tools

End-to-End Configuration Steps

SAP IAS – OKTA Integration

Step 1: Extract the IAS Tenant Metadata: Login IAS as Administrator -> Applications & Resources -> Tenant Settings -> Single Sign-On -> SAML 2.0 Configuration -> Download Metadata File

Step 2: Configure IAS Tenant in OKTA: Login OKTA Admin Portal as Administrator -> Click on the 'Use single sign on' - 'Add App' option -> Create New App -> Platform = Web | Sign on method = SAML 2.0 -> Create

Provide IAS Tenant Description -> Maintain Single Sign-On Endpoint URL from IAS (ACS End Point) as Single Sign-On URL |  Recipient URL | Destination URL. Audience Restriction -> IAS Domain. Name ID Format -> E-mail Address. Digest Algorithm -> SHA256 -> Save.

Navigate to the 'Sign On' tab -> Click on 'Identity Provider metadata' hyperlink -> Export Configuration Metadata into an XML file

Step 3: Configure OKTA as Trusted Corporate Identity Provider : Login IAS Portal as Administrator -> Click on Identity Providers -> Corporate Identity Providers -> Create

Go to SAML 2.0 Configuration -> Browse and upload the OKTA Metadata XML file -> Save

Step 4: SAP Datasphere - IAS Configuration:

Only a System Owner can perform Single Sign-On Administration activity in SAP Datasphere.

Assign the System Owner Privilege with valid email address

Navigate to Information -> Administration

Step 4.1: Go to Security Tab -> Click on Change icon -> Authentication Method -> Choose SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)

Step 4.2: Under SAML Single Sign-On (SSO) Configuration -> Download Service Provider Metadata -> Extract SAP Datasphere Tenant’s Metadata (Here SAP Datasphere is the Service Provider)

We will complete parallel configuration in IAS for SAP Datasphere and resume further steps for seamless verification process

Step 4.2.1: Configure SAP Datasphere Application in IAS

Open new browser session -> Login IAS Tenant as Administrator -> Applications & Resources -> Applications -> Create -> Provide Display Name -> Choose Type as SAP Datasphere Solution -> Protocol Type – SAML2.0

Go to SAML 2.0 Configuration -> Upload SAP Datasphere Metadata (Service Provider) extracted from Step 4.2

Maintain Subject Name Identifier with Email as Primary Attribute. It is optional to configure fallback attribute.

Default Name ID Format -> Email

By default, Conditional Authentication will be set with Identity Authentication as Default Identity Provider

Resuming Configuration in SAP Datasphere -> Administrator -> Security

 Step 4.3: Under SAML Single Sign-On (SSO) Configuration -> Upload Identity Provider Metadata -> Upload IAS Tenant Metadata extracted from Step 1

Step 4.4: Under Confirm that the mapping is working, provide the System Owner E-mail Address -> Verify Account -> Follow instructions as per prompt

Note: System Owner ID must exist in IAS with correct e-mail address maintained

Step 4.5: Click Save icon to enable SAML Single Sign-On -> Convert

Authentication Verification with IAS: Try login SAP Datasphere Tenant in an Incognito / Private Browser session. Authentication to happen by IAS.

 Enabling IAS as Proxy:

Go to Conditional Authentication of SAP Datasphere Application and change the Default Identity Provider to the OKTA Corporate Identity Provider created in Step 3.

Authentication Verification with IAS as Proxy : Try login SAP Datasphere Tenant in an Incognito / Private Browser session. Authentication to happen by OKTA via IAS.

Conclusion

Implementing SSO for SAP Datasphere using SAP Cloud Identity Services - SAP Identity Authentication Service provides a robust, scalable, and secure authentication framework.

By configuring IAS as Proxy for Corporate Identity Provider, organizations can achieve a unified authentication strategy that enhances both user experience and security posture.

Author’s Note

This implementation reflects a real-world enterprise approach to consolidating authentication mechanisms across SAP landscapes using modern identity services.

If you're planning your Single Sign-On (SSO) journey or optimizing SAP Applications Architecture, this model serves as a strong foundation.

Referral Links

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0002943651
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/connect-okta-to-identity-authentication/ba-p/13468665

#SAP #SingleSignOn #SSO #SAPDatasphere #SAPCloudIdentityServices #SAPIdentityAuthenticationService #SAPIAS #OKTA

entry.title: Transforming Manual Sales Order Processing into an Intelligent SAP BTP Workflow
entry.content:
Many organizations still rely on manual, email-driven sales order processing. Business users receive customer documents, extract key details, validate information, and create orders in SAP S/4HANA. While manageable at low volumes, this approach becomes time-consuming, error-prone, and difficult to scale as document complexity and volume increase.

Reimagining the Process with SAP BTP

This solution transforms the traditional process into an intelligent, end-to-end workflow using:

  • SAP Build Process Automation for orchestration
  • SAP Document AI for document understanding
  • SAP CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) for validation, enrichment, and user interaction

The result is a centralized, application-driven experience that minimizes manual effort while maintaining business control.

Solution Overview

The automated workflow replaces fragmented manual steps with a streamlined process:

  1. Incoming emails are monitored via SAP Build Process Automation
  2. Attachments are sent to SAP Document AI for data extraction
  3. Document AI extracts relevant sales order fields using a custom schema
  4. A CAP application retrieves and processes the extracted data
  5. Additional validation and enrichment is performed (e.g., master data checks, missing fields, amount validation)
  6. Business users review and approve data in a centralized interface before posting to SAP S/4HANA

Architecture Highlights

The solution is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP):

  • SAP Build Process Automation → event-driven orchestration
  • SAP Document AI → document extraction
  • SAP CAP → business logic, validation, enrichment, UI
  • SAP S/4HANA → system of record
  • Connectivity & Destinations → secure integration via APIs

This architecture enables scalable automation with human-in-the-loop control.

Why This Approach Matters

Document extraction alone is not enough. True automation requires business context, validation, and enrichment.

The CAP layer bridges this gap by:

  • Combining extracted data with SAP master data
  • Applying business rules and validations
  • Providing a controlled user review interface

This ensures accuracy while reducing manual effort.

Key Advantages

  • Reduced manual handoffs
  • Improved validation and enrichment control
  • Enhanced user experience through a centralized interface
  • Seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA
  • Scalable and future-ready architecture

Business Impact

This solution addresses the core challenge of converting unstructured documents into structured SAP transactions.

Customer results include:

  • ~30% increase in order-processing capacity
  • ~90% reduction in manual effort per transaction
  • Improved accuracy and reduced rework
  • Faster month-end and quarter-end close
  • Cleaner audit trail and compliance

Conclusion

By consolidating extraction, validation, enrichment, and user review into a single workflow, organizations can move from manual processing to intelligent automation - improving efficiency while maintaining control.

If you have any further questions, please leave a comment below or send your inquiries to the email CoE_AI_PlanningSpendExec@global.corp.sap. We will be happy to get back to you.

entry.title: Re: EU Pay Transparency in SAP SuccessFactors — A Suite-Level Compliance Playbook
entry.content:
Thank you for the amazing blog, 

We are currently working on our Pay Transparency project, with a key milestone of including salary ranges in job postings for Canada, the U.S., and Poland.

As we prepare for this, we are seeking SAP’s guidance and best practices on how to handle salary range displays for multi-region job requisitions.

Our Current Challenge:

We frequently post roles that are open to candidates across multiple regions (e.g., a "2-2" role open to all U.S. regions).

A significant challenge we’ve encountered is displaying accurate salary ranges for multi-region job requisitions. Currently, the system defaults to the primary region's range, but for compliance and transparency, we need to show the full range (lowest min to highest midpoint) for all applicable locations in a single posting.

Our Objective:

We are looking for a way to automate this process within SuccessFactors Recruiting/Talent Center to ensure it is streamlined, accurate, and error-free.

Could you please advise on a recommended approach or best practice for this scenario? Specifically, we are interested in any "out-of-the-box" automation or upcoming features that might simplify the management of these wide-ranging salary displays.

entry.title: Recording and slide deck of SAP on IBM i April webcast available for download
entry.content:
In our SAP on IBM i Webcast "Inject More Security into your IBM i SAP servers", we presented a number of SQL queries to identify vulnerabilities that are available through IBM Access Client Solutions (ACS) as sample queries. We showed how to use these queries on your IBM i server to identify user profiles with too much special authorities, libraries and objects that can be used or even modified by anyone on your system and other security risks. We gave you some examples, what to expect on an IBM i server running SAP NetWeaver software, so that you can take action if your findings differ from the expectations.

As usual, the recording and slide deck of the webcast have been made available for download at the link https://ibm.biz/RadioSAPIBMi. You can find it there in the folder 2026, subfolder April. If you missed the webcast or want to look at some aspects in more detail, you can look at the provided material and watch the recording at your own speed.

The link is accessible by invitation only. If you are not yet a user of Box, it will be necessary to register (free of charge) at Box with the company email address that will be used for the download. If you were registered for the webcast, you should already be authorized to it. If this is the first time you are trying to download material from this link, or if your access was lost for whatever reason, you can request access by sending an email to ibmsapevents@ibm.com and providing your name, company name, country, requested company email address (if different from sender), as well as the requested webcast date and title.

entry.title: Re: Issue Processing Task 0030 (Balancing of Totals Trans...
entry.content:
Hi, I have resolved the issue and am sharing the solution here in case it helps others encountering the same problem.

POSDM Balancing Configuration – Invoice vs Day Totals
I was able to resolve the balancing issue in POSDM through configuration. Sharing the steps below for anyone facing a similar problem:

Step 1: Define Rule for Invoice-Level Balancing
Navigate to:
IMG → POSDM → POS Inbound Processing → Tasks → Define Rules

Assign 0001 in the field:
“Filter val. f, BAdI for rule type 0”
This rule ensures balancing between sales line totals and tender totals at the invoice level.
Step 2: Assign Rule to Task
Assign the above rule to Task  0030(Balancing Task).
This enables the system to validate balancing for each individual transaction (invoice-wise).
Step 3: Assign Task to Task Group
Include Task 0030 in the relevant Task Group used during inbound processing.
 Alternative: Day-Level Balancing
If you want to validate daily totals instead of invoice-level totals:

In the same rule configuration, replace:
0001→0003
This will configure the system to check balancing at the day level (aggregated totals).
Thank you.

entry.title: 🇵🇱 Hands-on with CAP CDS
entry.content:
This CodeJam gives you a thorough grounding in CAP CDS modelling.

In this hands-on workshop you'll become familiar and comfortable with the key features of CDS modelling, primarily focusing on the Conceptual Definition Language (CDL), the predominantly declarative domain-specific language (DSL) in the CDS family. You'll explore the different concepts that make CAP such a compelling framework for today's world of AI, where concision, correctness and the imperative to write less code is at the forefront of everyone's mind.

No prior experience is required, and this workshop is suitable for non-developers as well as developers. All you need is curiosity and a desire to learn!

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is on Thu 21 May 2026 in Kraków, PL, kindly hosted (including lunch & refreshments) by GE HealthCare, thank you very much! (See below for specific arrival instructions).

⚠️ Note that capacity is currently limited to 18 people, so please ensure you use the RSVP links on this page accordingly. We will update this limitation if the room circumstances and availability changes between now and the event.

The instructor is Developer Advocate DJ Adams. 

There are essential prerequisites that must be completed by every attendee, before arriving at the event location.

Specific arrival instructions: After entering the building, please sign in with your name at the journal. Then turn right and proceed to the 2nd floor, where reception will issue your guest badge."

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud - Cannot remove configuration - cust...
entry.content:
Hello @Kraikanchit,

in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud not all customizing settings can be deleted.

 

👉Please see KBA Note 3381770 - Delete entries from configuration activity (SSCUI) - SAP for Me.

In this note you can also find an excel file which lists all customizing activities.

 

As for your specific request 103530 Define Condition Groups: deletion is not supported.

This is why you can not see any delete button.

A possible workaround is to re-name them to "Do not Use". However, they will still stay available in your system.

 

I hope this helps.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Instructor‑Led Training Now Available: Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance
entry.content:
Following our recent post announcing the new Learning Journey Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance, we would like to remind you that the corresponding instructor‑led Training is already open for registration and coming up soon.

SAP Business AI is shaping how Finance processes are designed and executed today. While the Learning Journey offers flexible, self‑paced access to this topic, the instructor‑led Training provides an opportunity to deepen this knowledge in a guided, hands‑on setting with direct expert interaction.

Instructor‑led Training: Applying Business AI in SAP Finance

The two‑day instructor‑led Training focuses on the practical application of SAP Business AI in Finance and is designed for consultants and finance professionals who want to actively work with these capabilities.

During the training, participants will:

  • Apply SAP Joule in concrete Finance processes and scenarios
  • Work hands‑on with SAP Joule Agents to support more complex Finance activities
  • Explore consultant‑focused SAP Joule use cases for analysis, explanation, and solution design
  • Gain practical insights into Finance functionality within SAP Business Data Cloud
  • Understand the technical and functional foundation needed to position and demonstrate AI in SAP Finance

The course places strong emphasis on interactive exercises, live demonstrations, and real‑world Finance examples, allowing participants to directly transfer what they learn into project and customer contexts.

Instructor‑led Training details:

  • Duration: 2 full days
  • Delivery format: Hybrid, with on‑site participation in Walldorf and virtual attendance
  • Upcoming session: June 10–11
  • Language: German for this session; English sessions are planned and will be announced

Registration link: F0610 - Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance | SAP Training

You’re also welcome to check out our new Business AI Learning Journey: Applying Business AI Solutions and Expertise in SAP Finance
 
Both the Learning Journey and the instructor‑led Training stand on their own and do not require completion of the other. They can be taken individually or combined to deepen Business AI expertise in SAP Finance.
Both formats support the development of practical, role‑relevant AI expertise in SAP Finance.

We look forward to your participation.

entry.title: Sales Tax Calculation for Free Item and Tax amount to be borne by seller
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have requirement from India based client related to Free Goods. 

They run free schemes like 10+1 Free. GST tax to be paid for the Free item as per Government rule. As per Client requirement, tax will be paid by the seller and not to be charged to the customer for Free item.  They are using S4 HANA PUC. Using CBXN item category for free goods.

Example: Saleable Goods Price - 100, Qty - 10, Order Amount - 1000, Tax - 50

Free Goods Sale Price - 100, Qty -1, Sales Value - 100, Tax - 5. Discount for Customer - 105. 

We tried to achieve by applying standard 100% discount condition before calculation of tax at item level. This will make the tax rate 0 which is not legally acceptable.

We tried to achieve by applying standard 100% discount condition after calculation of tax at item level. But this is driving the Order Item Net value (negative) -5 with Tax amount as 5. This cannot be submitted to GST.

If the discount applied at order header level (with additional Routines), then it will be distributed to all line items as per standard which is not acceptable by the client.

Please suggest.

Regards,

Krishna

entry.title: Re: Finished product processing
entry.content:
Hello @venkat_v 

Just to it by the book and model in the system real life activities, i.e.

  1. Goods receipt of a raw material
  2. Quality inspection
  3. Consumption of raw material only after the quality inspection
  4. Production confirmation
  5. Finished product receipt

Don't cut corners, don't allow for not inspected raw material consumption, post finished product receipt only after raw material consumption. The system does all of that out of the box. 

If you skip quality control due to business "urgency", be honest to yourself and skip quality inspection in the system. Don't post in the system any dummy/virtual quality control that you don't do in real life.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: how to import primary form item in data transfer work...
entry.content:
Hi @MarlonG ,

This has to be imported using the OCTF template, alongside OJDT/JDT1. On DTW, the path is Transactional Data > Financials > Journal Entries > PrimaryFormItems

You can get this template in DTW itself or browsing DTW's template folder .

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Transforming Healthcare IT Through Integration: SAP’s Comprehensive Ecosystem Approach
entry.content:
As healthcare systems worldwide face mounting pressures to deliver better patient outcomes while managing costs and operational efficiency, the need for intelligent, integrated solutions has never been greater.

SAP’s mission for healthcare is straightforward yet ambitious: help healthcare companies run more efficiently and deliver high-quality, value-based care. This commitment drives our product development and partnership strategies, ensuring our ecosystem solutions address real-world healthcare challenges while enabling organizations to focus on patient care.

From SAP Healthcare Product Management perspective, we are driving four interconnected themes that create our comprehensive ecosystem for modern healthcare transformation. In this blog post, I’ll outline these themes that define our work and provide insights that serve as your foundation for understanding our healthcare innovation approach.

Verticalization of the SAP Business Suite

The SAP Business Suite represents a comprehensive, integrated ecosystem of our cloud-based applications including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Concur, and other solutions, all built on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). This integrated approach ensures that applications work together seamlessly along end-to-end business processes, generating trusted business-critical data at its source and leveraging the SAP Business Data Cloud for unified insights and AI-driven capabilities through SAP Business AI and SAP Joule.

For healthcare providers such as hospitals and health systems, verticalization of the SAP Business Suite is crucial to address the unique operational complexities inherent in healthcare delivery. Healthcare organizations require specialized adaptations across multiple areas, such as e.g., regulatory compliance frameworks, quality management systems, workforce management for clinical staff, and analytics that align with healthcare-specific KPIs. Additionally, regional requirements add another layer of complexity- healthcare providers must often comply with country-specific reporting standards for public health institutions or local regulatory frameworks.

The partner ecosystem plays a vital role in delivering this verticalization, as partners bring deep industry expertise, regional knowledge, and specialized solutions that complement SAP’s core platform capabilities. Through collaborative innovation, partners develop industry-specific extensions, address local compliance requirements, and create solutions that bridge the gap between standard business processes and healthcare-specific workflows.

Within the Cloud ERP component of the SAP Business Suite, SAP has developed industry partner opportunity maps that provide clear guidance on priority areas for innovation and collaboration. These maps help partners identify where they can make the greatest impact and seize growth opportunities within specific industries. A dedicated healthcare opportunity map is now available, designed to foster innovation specifically for healthcare providers. This map will continue to evolve and expand based on ongoing feedback from customers and partners to ensure it addresses the most critical needs in the healthcare sector. PartnerEdge partners can access these comprehensive opportunity maps in the PartnerEdge Portal.

We’re also working on verticalizing the SAP BTP and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC). Central to this collaborative approach is the support of HL7® FHIR® (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources), the vital healthcare data standard that enables seamless information exchange across systems. SAP Health Data Services for FHIR exemplifies how we’re providing healthcare-specific capabilities that enable partners to build sophisticated solutions more rapidly and reliably.

Patient Administration and Accounting

SAP has strategically evolved its healthcare approach to offer customers enhanced flexibility through multiple transition pathways to next-generation solutions. To support this transformation, SAP provides various options that leverage our partner ecosystem and modern technology stack, allowing organizations to choose the path that best aligns with their specific needs and implementation strategies.

Customers can start their transition by integrating IS-H with Cloud ERP Private (S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition) as an initial step in their digital transformation journey. This approach serves as a bridge solution, allowing organizations to modernize their ERP foundation while maintaining their existing patient management capabilities. The documentation for configuring the IS-H to SAP S/4HANA integration can be found here.

Another or also subsequent pathway leverages SAP partners who offer S/4HANA Add-On solutions specifically designed to serve as successors to SAP Patient Management. Here, SAP has partnered with leading healthcare technology providers to evolve SAP Patient Management (IS-H) capabilities onto a modern technological foundation. These partners have built upon the proven IS-H functionality, migrating it to the advanced S/4HANA platform while enhancing it with innovative features aligned with their specialized product strategies. This approach also supports a structured migration path where custom development can be systematically assessed, refactored, and integrated into the new platform architecture. Partners leverage proven methodologies and tools to analyze existing custom code, identify reusable components, and provide clear transformation strategies that minimize disruption while modernizing the technical foundation. This allows valuable business logic and processes developed over years of IS-H implementation to be preserved and enhanced rather than rebuilt from scratch. In summary, this collaborative approach empowers customers with next-generation solutions that combine SAP’s established healthcare expertise on top of modern technology, partner-driven innovations. These solutions can be found in the SAP Store.

Finally, customers can fully embrace a comprehensive Hospital Information System (HIS) approach, either as a complete single system or through modular solutions that include billing and administrative functionality. This option represents the most transformative pathway, where organizations can leverage advanced HIS solutions, which provide integrated patient administration and accounting capabilities alongside clinical functionality. These systems can serve as complete replacements for traditional IS-H implementations while offering modern integration capabilities with Cloud ERP through standardized APIs and the SAP Business Technology Platform.

All these transition options are designed to ensure investment protection, support clean core principles, and leverage open standards for interoperability, providing customers with flexible pathways based on their specific strategy, boundary conditions, and regional requirements.

Any-EMR

Electronic Medical Records represent the most critical systems in healthcare organizations today, containing comprehensive clinical data and driving essential workflows throughout the patient journey. While the healthcare industry is evolving toward more modular, specialized system architectures and AI-ready data platforms, EMRs currently remain the foundational hub for clinical operations in most healthcare settings. Understanding both this current reality and the emerging trend towards system modularization, SAP’s Any-EMR strategy focuses on enabling seamless, standardized integration with EMR vendors to create modern, future-proof end-to-end experiences.

This approach is designed to eliminate the system dependencies and process bottlenecks that currently plague healthcare operations, while positioning organizations to adapt flexibly as the industry continues its gradual shift toward more modular healthcare technology ecosystems. This strategy extends beyond technical integration - we actively welcome vendors to join the SAP PartnerEdge program, enabling them to leverage our technology stack, collaborate in customer engagement, while contributing to a more connected healthcare ecosystem. Please find the currently available solutions in the SAP Store here as well.

As depicted below, the “integration needs” between healthcare systems and the SAP portfolio span the complete patient journey: registration, treatment documentation, coding, billing and denial management, clinical supply chain operations, and analytics.

 

Our technical approach leverages industry standards, particularly HL7 FHIR, to provide consistent and reliable connections across different EMR systems. SAP Health Data Services for FHIR serves as the foundation, offering a Platform-as-a-Service implementation of the HL7 FHIR standard on SAP BTP that manages health data using healthcare domain models while providing REST and messaging APIs for seamless connectivity.

SAP Integration Suite plays a pivotal role in orchestrating the complete Any-EMR integration ecosystem. Acting as the central integration hub, it connects healthcare provider systems with Cloud ERP and other SAP Business Suite applications through standardized Healthcare APIs. Together with SAP Health Data Services for FHIR the integration Suite manages the complex bi-directional data flows, ensuring data consistency and process alignment across the healthcare and the SAP landscape.

The Integration Suite’s capabilities are enhanced by comprehensive FHIR Integration Packages that are specifically designed for healthcare scenarios. These packages, accessible through the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, include integration flows, implementation guides, and best practices derived from SAP’s own integration experience.

The value proposition extends beyond technical integration to deliver improved user experiences through single sign-on capabilities, unified interfaces, and elimination of duplicate data entry. Real-time integration ensures immediate data synchronization, while pre-built integration packages available through our Business Accelerator Hub significantly reduce implementation timelines and project complexity.

Finally, when applications or modules are connected at a business process level, synergies may emerge that create additional value beyond the individual capabilities of each system. The result is a multiplicative effect where the integrated solution delivers significantly more value than the sum of its parts. This transforms isolated systems into a cohesive ecosystem that reduces operational friction and enhances decision-making.

Innovation Through Ecosystem Collaboration and AI

The future of healthcare innovation lies in a unified data and AI strategy that transforms how organizations harness their most valuable asset - their data. The SAP Business Data Cloud represents a pivotal evolution in this approach, centralizing, harmonizing, and enriching data from both SAP and non-SAP healthcare systems to create a single source of truth that becomes the fuel for advanced AI-driven insights and decision-making. This integrated foundation enables healthcare organizations to enhance patient outcomes, streamline administrative processes, and deploy predictive analytics for better resource management.

Healthcare innovation requires collaboration across the entire ecosystem, which is why SAP partners with organizations throughout the industry through our Business Technology Platform. BTP serves as both an extension platform for customers and a foundational platform for partners, providing comprehensive capabilities spanning application development, automation, data analytics, integration services, artificial intelligence, and platform foundation services.

For customers currently on their digital transformation journey, this creates immediate value - they can connect existing systems like SAP Patient Management to SAP BTP using the HL7 FHIR standard, gradually incorporating intelligent features while protecting their current investments.

Looking ahead, we plan to expand SAP Health Data Services for FHIR to include primary healthcare data products covering administrative and financial data in the coming months. When available, this will make health-related data based on the FHIR domain model natively accessible within SAP Business Data Cloud and, hence, create new opportunities for analytics and AI-powered insights that can transform healthcare delivery and operations. 

Technical Foundation and Clean Core Approach

All transformation pathways emphasize SAP’s clean core philosophy, which ensures that organizations can protect their technology investments while enabling future extensibility and innovation. This approach relies on open standards for interoperability, with SAP Business Technology Platform serving as the integration foundation for all approaches. The clean core methodology supports multiple EMR environments with harmonized integration approaches, ensuring that organizations can adapt and evolve their technology landscapes without being locked into proprietary architectures.

The SAP Integration Suite provides comprehensive connectivity capabilities, while the Business Accelerator Hub offers pre-built integration packages and APIs accessible at api.sap.com. This standardized approach reduces implementation complexity while ensuring that all integrations follow best practices for security, compliance, and performance.

Looking Forward

SAP’s healthcare strategy positions us and our partners to address the evolving needs of healthcare organizations worldwide. By combining our robust technology platform with deep industry expertise and strong partner relationships, we’re creating an ecosystem where innovation thrives and healthcare organizations can focus on what matters most: delivering exceptional patient care.

The future of healthcare is intelligent, integrated, and collaborative. With this approach, we’re not just adapting to this future - we’re actively shaping it through comprehensive solutions that transform how healthcare organizations operate, integrate, and innovate.

 

HL7, FHIR, and the Flame Design mark are registered trademarks of Health Level Seven International.

For deeper insights into our FHIR strategy and integration approaches, explore our related blog posts:

  • Benefits of the HL7 FHIR Industry Standard
  • Integration from IS-H to SAP Health Data Services for FHIR
  • Integrating EMR Systems with SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Healthcare integration - Patient as a business partner in SAP Cloud ERP Private

entry.title: I built a zero-dependency Excel plugin for SAP CAP — does this solve a problem you have too?
entry.content:
Every time I needed to generate .xlsx files in a CAP project the answer was the same: pull in exceljs or xlsx, wire up the API, and deal with the bundle weight. I decided to solve it differently.

I built @cap-js/excel — a CAP plugin that generates Excel workbooks using only Node.js built-ins (zlib and buffer). No external runtime dependencies at all.

It's not public yet. Before publishing I want to know if this is just a problem I have or if it resonates with more people.


HOW IT WORKS

Install the package and it auto-discovers itself as a CAP plugin — no manual require in your handlers. The full API is available under cds.excel.


Entity-driven tables — the part I use most

The thing that saves the most time: pass a CDS entity and the plugin builds the columns automatically. It reads your entity's elements, skips associations and compositions, and generates one column per primitive field. It picks up @title and @Common.Label annotations as column headers and maps CDS types to the right Excel cell styles out of the box.

    srv.on('exportReport', async (req) => {
      const { ExcelBuilder } = cds.excel
      const rows = await SELECT.from('MyService.Orders').orderBy('createdAt desc')

      const wb = new ExcelBuilder()
      wb.addSheet('Orders').addEntity(srv.entities.Orders, {
        rows,
        labels:     true,    // column headers from @title / @Common.Label
        autoFormat: true,    // integer, decimal, date styles from CDS types
        exclude:    ['internalField', 'technicalKey'],
        order:      ['orderDate', 'customer', 'total'],
        overrides:  { total: { header: 'Total (USD)' } },
        theme:      'blue',
      })

      return wb
    })

CDS type to Excel style mapping:

Integer, Int16, Int32, Int64, UInt8 → Whole number
Decimal, Double → 2 decimal places
Date, DateTime, Timestamp → Short date
Everything else → General

If you prefer incremental configuration there is a fluent EntityBuilder that defers rendering until serialisation time:

const cfg = sheet.getEntity(srv.entities.Orders)
cfg
.setLabels(true)
.setAutoFormat(true)
.include(['orderDate', 'customer', 'amount', 'status'])
.setOrder(['orderDate', 'customer'])
.addRows(rows)

Pair it with the @Excel.export annotation and the download becomes fully declarative — annotate the action in your .cds model, return the ExcelBuilder instance from the handler, and the plugin intercepts the result and sends the binary response automatically:

    // in your .cds model
    @Excel.export: { filename: 'orders.xlsx' }
    action exportOrders();

    // in your handler
    srv.on('exportOrders', async (req) => {
      const wb = new ExcelBuilder()
      wb.addSheet('Orders').addEntity(srv.entities.Orders, {
        rows:       await SELECT.from('MyService.Orders'),
        labels:     true,
        autoFormat: true,
      })
      return wb  // plugin handles the binary response
    })
 

Tests:

This is not mocked or manually created — it is a direct output from the current implementation.


Other features

- Multi-sheet workbooks — add as many named sheets as you need per workbook
- 8 colour themes (blue, green, orange, red, purple, dark, light, default) with header rows and alternating stripes
- Formula support — SUM, PRODUCT, cell multiply, and arbitrary inline formulas
- Three download patterns — Base64 string for OData actions, direct binary via cds.excel.download(), or fully declarative via @Excel.export
- Worksheet protection — sheet.protect({ password }) to prevent accidental edits
- Sensitivity labels — embed classification metadata as custom document properties
- Structured logging — integrates with cds.log('excel'), falls back to console.* outside CAP
- Deterministic output — fixed timestamps produce byte-identical ZIP archives across builds
- Standalone — works without CAP via require('@cap-js/excel')


WHY NO DEPENDENCIES?

exceljs is around 200 KB minified and pulls sub-dependencies. xlsx has a dual license that can get complicated in corporate environments. Since .xlsx is just a ZIP of XML files with an open spec (OOXML / ECMA-376), implementing the essentials with Node's native zlib is doable — and it removes any supply chain, license, or bundle-size conversation entirely.


IS THIS USEFUL TO YOU?

A few questions before I decide whether to publish:

- Do you use CAP and need to generate Excel today? How are you solving it?
- Does the zero-dependency angle matter in your context — audits, license policies, bundle size?
- Is there a feature that would make this actually useful for you that isn't listed here?

Happy to share more details or a demo. Any feedback appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Order Open on Month-End Not Considered for WIP – Part...
entry.content:
Why the order was not picked up in March WIP is because of the following:

Order status on 31st March:
The order was technically open, but WIP calculation (CO88H) looks at costs posted up to the cutoff date and whether the order is in a WIP‑relevant status.

Partial delivery on 1st April:
That means as of 31st March there was no delivery posted, so the system saw no unfinished costs to capitalize as WIP. The first delivery happened only after the period closed.

Variance calculation on 1st April:
Once variances are calculated, the system assumes the order is moving toward closure. If settlement is run in April, the March WIP run will not retroactively include that order.

TECO on 2nd April:
After TECO, the order is no longer WIP‑relevant; only variances are settled.

To fix this going forward, align the sequence in such a way to post costs → run WIP → settle → close order.

entry.title: What is new in SAP Service Cloud v2 in Q1/2026
entry.content:
What’s New in SAP Service Cloud (Q1/2026)

Explore a selection of our new features in this blog post and join our webcasts for live demos and deeper insights SAP Service Cloud Version 2 updates.  Register for our Q1 2026 customer webcast here. 

We continue to advance SAP Service Cloud with innovations designed to improve service efficiency through seamless collaboration between AI capabilities and human agents. By enabling smooth transitions from AI-driven self-service to human-led case resolution, improving visibility into different types of service assignments and simplifying case and complaint handling, these updates help service teams work more efficiently while reducing customer effort.

Looking ahead to SAP Sapphire, we will be sharing additional AI innovations that further expand AI–human collaboration in customer service and introduce new capabilities for intelligent, scalable service experiences.

 

Digital Service Agent: From conversational self-service to seamless case creation

Last year, we launched the Digital Service Agent to support conversational self-service.  If self-service doesn’t resolve the issue, customers can easily move to live support or create a case in SAP Service Cloud. During the conversation, the Digital Service Agent captures the key information provided by the customer. This is transferred to SAP Service Cloud, where the case is created with the relevant context.

This seamless transition from self-service to case creation reduces customer effort and saves time. Service teams receive a ready-to-handle case with the captured information, enabling them to continue the resolution process more efficiently.

SAP Digital Service Agent -case creation

Agent Inbox: Prioritize assignments with a consolidated view

Service agents typically work across multiple assignment types, and having a clear view of different tasks and priorities is important for efficient work. The new Agent Inbox consolidates these work items (cases, service orders, tasks) into a single view within the agent workspace, complemented by visual graphs that provide an overview of workload and priorities. This makes it easier for service representatives to quickly identify the most relevant and urgent assignments.

By bringing all assignments together in one place, agents spend less time navigating between different areas and can focus more on resolving customer issues, helping improve overall service team productivity.

SAP Service Cloud Agent Inbox

 

Case Management

To support efficient AI–human collaboration, we continue to enhance core Case Management capabilities in SAP Service Cloud. These improvements focus on simplifying daily work for service reps, providing better process control, and helping accelerate case resolution.


Case Management: enhanced approvals 

Based on customer input, we have introduced several approval enhancements. Service reps can now easily see the current approval status directly in the case header, providing better visibility into ongoing approval steps. Approval status can also be used in autoflow scenarios, enabling more structured and automated case handling.

In addition, the case UI can be set to read-only during approval, helping prevent unintended changes and ensuring process consistency while approvals are in progress. Notifications or emails can also be triggered based on approval status, keeping approvers and service teams informed and helping move cases forward more efficiently.

SAP Service Cloud Case Management approvals


Case Management: Simplified complaint handling

Complaint handling is a critical part of the customer experience, where efficient processes and clear visibility are key to resolving issues effectively. We have simplified the complaint handling process, and service reps can now capture complaints for specific items within a case. They can create complaint items directly from individual items in a sales order and record details such as quantity, unit of measure, and additional attributes. This is available for customers using both SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud.

For example, if a customer raises a complaint about a specific product within a larger sales order, the service rep can create a case for that individual item and manage it using the standard case process. Complaint items can also be created for non-referenced product items.

Service teams can also easily create follow-up actions, such as creating a new sales order with a discount, directly from the complaint. These follow-up documents are created within SAP Service Cloud and seamlessly integrated with SAP S/4HANA, helping ensure a smooth end-to-end complaint resolution process.

This enables more detailed complaint tracking and improves visibility into product performance and complaint trends. Service teams can also trigger follow-up actions more efficiently, helping accelerate complaint resolution while supporting better analysis and decision-making.

SAP Service Cloud Case Management Complaint Handling

Utilities customer service -improved visibility and deeper integrations
 

We continue to enhance utilities customer service capabilities by providing service reps with better visibility into operational data and customer context.  

With the introduction of AMI meter ping history, service reps can now view real-time and historical meter status information, enabling them to answer questions about meter connectivity and operational states directly during customer interactions.  

We have also added support for managing bank data using IBAN, allowing service reps to create and maintain bank details for contract accounts more easily, either by entering the IBAN directly or deriving it from standard bank information.

In addition, service orders replicated from SAP S/4HANA are now associated with the premise addresses, allowing service reps to quickly identify service locations without relying on technical identifiers such as equipment IDs or device location codes.  

These enhancements provide additional visibility and help streamline key service processes for utilities teams.

More information

For more details on the above release features, check out the following resources and register to our webinar

  • SAP Service Cloud V2 What’s New  
  • SAP Service Cloud V2 Release Navigator 
  • Q1/2026 release webcast

entry.title: Bringing Analytics to Life with Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst
entry.content:
For more than 15 years, Université de Sherbrooke has been a member of the SAP University Alliances program, and our School of Management has continuously benefited from this global academic community to innovate in the teaching of enterprise systems. Over the years, SAP technologies have allowed us to move beyond theory and immerse students in realistic, hands-on learning experiences. With the launch of Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst, this pedagogical journey has taken an exciting new turn, one that immediately resonated with our students.

From the classroom to the rink: a natural fit

When we first introduced Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst in class, one of the most striking aspects was how easy it was to deploy. As a free, cloud-based solution built on SAP Analytics Cloud, the game required no complex setup, no local installations, and no steep learning curve. Within a single class session, students were able to log in, explore dashboards, and begin analyzing data. This simplicity made it easy to integrate the game seamlessly into existing courses. 

The hockey context immediately grabbed attention. Even students who were not die-hard fans quickly became engaged, as the scenario provided an intuitive way to understand abstract analytics concepts. The connection between data, performance metrics, and decision-making became tangible almost instantly.

Student reactions: curiosity turns into engagement

The reaction from students was overwhelmingly positive. What began as curiosity (“We’re analyzing NHL data in class?”) quickly turned into deep engagement. The competitive and playful nature of the game encouraged participation, discussion, and collaboration. Perhaps most importantly, the game lowered the intimidation factor often associated with analytics. Students who initially felt less confident with data became more comfortable experimenting, exploring visualizations, and asking questions. 

What students actually learned

Beyond engagement, the learning outcomes were clear. Students developed core analytics competencies, including data interpretation, KPI analysis, and storytelling with dashboards. Because everything is grounded in SAP Analytics Cloud, students also gained hands-on experience with a professional-grade analytics platform used by thousands of organizations worldwide. This connection between academic learning and real business tools significantly enhanced the perceived relevance of the activity.

The availability of multiple versions (novice and advanced), with different gameplay scenarios (goal scoring, goalies) also allowed us to adapt the experience to different levels, from introductory analytics courses to more advanced classes.

Advice for fellow educators

For educators considering Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst, my main advice is simple: start small and let the game do the work. You don’t need to redesign an entire course to benefit from it. Even a short in-class activity can spark curiosity and meaningful discussions. Encourage students to explore, make mistakes, and reflect on their decisions rather than focusing solely on “winning” the game.

Finally, embrace the storytelling aspect. The real power of this approach lies in how it connects data, technology, and human decision-making within a narrative students care about.

A powerful addition to analytics education

Business Builders – The Hockey Analyst represents a compelling example of how partnerships between academia, industry, and sports organizations (SAP, the NHL, and the ERPsim Lab at HEC Montréal) can produce innovative, engaging, and pedagogically sound learning experiences. For us at Université de Sherbrooke, it has become a valuable tool to enrich analytics education, spark student motivation, and bridge the gap between classroom learning and real-world practice.

-Prof. Olivier Caya, Université de Sherbrooke (Canada)

entry.title: Automatic Reassignment of Cases after Employee Logs out in Service Cloud v2
entry.content:
Problem: Our client will go live with the Service Cloud v2 but is currently using a tool that has following logic implemented out of the box:
Key Logic and Features for Automatic Reassignment

  • Reroute on Logout: The system allows for the automatic reassignment of interactions from an agent who has logged out, placing them back into the queue for redistribution to the best available agent based on skill, category, and tags.
  • Availability Management: If an agent logs out, they are instantly marked as unavailable for automatic assignment, ensuring new cases are not routed to them.
  • The logic is as follows:
    1.  Agent logs out: The agent session terminates, updating the agent's state in the system to "offline" or "non-routable"
    2. Case Analysis: The system identifies open cases that were assigned to the logged-out agent.
    3. Automatic Reassignment: The system triggers a reroute action, moving the cases back into the queue.
    4. Redistribution: The intelligent router picks the next best available agent in the corresponding group to take over the case

Our client sees this as an absolute must-have for the MVP scope. Are there any viable solutions to how this can be configured in the V2 or has anyone in the community faced similar issues and found good solutions for this?
Any support is highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: ODBC connection failure
entry.content:
The server name is not the host or IP name but the Server Name as defined by the first -n <name> in the engine start line. If there is no -n, the Server Name will be the same as the Database Name. Also, it must be the network server dbsrv17 unless the client is on the same same machine (which simplifies the required connection parameters generally)

Example 1: Recommended server and database naming using -n

Server:  dbsrv17 -n MyServerName database.db -n MyDatabaseName

The connection parameters from a client would be:

        host: <machine_or_ip>

        port:

        server name: MyServerName

         database name: MyDatabaseName

Example 2: No naming specified, using the defaults

Server:  dbsrv17 database.db

In this case, the server and database names are based on the database file without the extension

The connection parameters from a client would be:

        host: <machine_or_ip>

        port:

        server name: database

         database name: database

A more complete discussion of Connections and Connection strings can be found here Database Connections | SAP Help Portal
